title: iptables / nftables
description: Linux kernel firewall framework — filters, NATs, and mangles network packets using rule chains.
fullExplanation: iptables is the userspace tool for configuring the Linux kernel's netfilter packet filtering framework. Rules are organized into tables (filter, nat, mangle, raw) and chains (INPUT, OUTPUT, FORWARD, PREROUTING, POSTROUTING). Each packet traverses the relevant chains and is matched against rules in order — the first matching rule's target (ACCEPT, DROP, REJECT, LOG) is applied. nftables is the modern replacement for iptables, with a cleaner syntax and better performance.

tables.description: Network Address Translation — DNAT, SNAT, MASQUERADE
Connection tracking bypass — for high-performance rules

commonRules.description: Allow return traffic for established connections
Default deny — drop all other inbound traffic

keyFacts: Rules processed in order — first match wins
Stateful tracking: -m state --state ESTABLISHED,RELATED allows return traffic
nftables: modern replacement — better performance, atomic rule updates, cleaner syntax
ufw (Uncomplicated Firewall): simplified iptables frontend for Ubuntu
firewalld: zone-based firewall management for RHEL/CentOS
iptables-save / iptables-restore: persist rules across reboots
